forecast-period.text: A broad trough lies over SA. A cold front clips the southeast as an upper trough moves up over SA Wednesday and Thursday. A high pressure system develops south of the Bight late in the week and persists over the weekend.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
South to southwesterly about 10 knots.
Below 0.5 metres.
Southwesterly 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. The chance of a thunderstorm in the west.
Southerly about 10 knots increasing to 15 to 20 knots in the early morning.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres around midday.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Cloudy. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Southerly 15 to 25 knots increasing to 20 to 30 knots in the late afternoon then decreasing to 20 to 25 knots in the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Cloudy.
Southerly 20 to 25 knots turning southeasterly 15 to 20 knots during the day.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing below 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres.
Cloudy.
